evals.name: standard-resignation-4-week-notice
evals.prompt: Our Senior Product Manager, Aisha Khan, has resigned. She's given 4 weeks notice and her last day is April 18th. She manages relationships with 3 key enterprise clients and owns the product roadmap. No successor has been identified yet. I need a full offboarding plan.
evals.expected_output: A four-phase offboarding plan covering immediate actions, handover planning, exit interview, and last-day checklist — with specific dates, owners, and knowledge transfer priorities that ensure no client relationships or product knowledge falls through the cracks.
expectations: Covers all four phases: immediate actions (within 24 hours), handover planning (complete by Week 2), exit interview (Week 3), and last-day checklist — skipping any phase creates gaps that surface as crises after the employee leaves

Exit interview is scheduled for Week 3 (not Week 1 and not the final week) and conducted by HR, not the line manager — employees will not be honest about management issues to the manager they are leaving
Handover plan completion is targeted for Week 2, not the final week — the skill explicitly warns that departing employees are mentally disengaged in their last week; quality drops sharply

Includes IT access removal as a specific checklist with systems enumerated — the skill warns to never leave access removal to chance; every system, account, and credential must be documented for revocation

Triggers knowledge capture (references /knowledge skill) for the enterprise client relationships and product roadmap — these are high-risk knowledge areas where the departing employee is likely the sole holder
Flags '[MANAGER INPUT REQUIRED]' for successor identification since none has been identified — the plan should not guess at succession; it should flag the gap explicitly for the manager to resolve
Output begins with the mandatory header block including CONFIDENTIAL sensitivity — offboarding plans contain departure reasons and access information that must be handled securely

evals.name: offboarding-with-existing-knowledge-capture
evals.prompt: We're offboarding our Head of Data Engineering, Raj Patel. His last day is May 2nd, 6 weeks notice. We've already run /knowledge and have a capture plan in progress — 3 sessions scheduled covering data pipeline architecture, vendor contracts, and team processes. I need the rest of the offboarding plan: handover, exit interview, IT access, and last-day logistics.
evals.expected_output: An offboarding plan that integrates with the existing knowledge capture work rather than duplicating it — the handover timeline aligns with the capture sessions, and the exit interview is positioned to capture organisational feedback that the knowledge sessions would miss.
expectations: References and integrates with the existing /knowledge capture plan rather than creating a duplicate knowledge capture workflow — the skill's BEFORE IMPLEMENTATION table explicitly checks for prior /knowledge output
Handover plan timeline aligns with the 3 scheduled knowledge capture sessions — the handover documents should build on what the capture sessions produce, not run in parallel asking the same questions

Exit interview questions focus on organisational feedback (what should change, what to protect) rather than technical knowledge — the technical knowledge is already being captured in the /knowledge sessions; the exit interview serves a different purpose

IT access removal checklist is comprehensive for a Head of Data Engineering role — this likely includes database admin access, cloud platform credentials, CI/CD pipeline access, data warehouse admin, and potentially third-party SaaS tools
Includes rehire eligibility documentation in the last-day checklist — the skill's universal rules state: never close an offboarding without confirming the rehire decision is documented
All checklist items have specific dates and owners assigned — the skill requires this; an unowned action is an action that will not happen

evals.name: negative-onboarding-request
evals.prompt: We have a new developer starting next week. Can you create an onboarding plan with their first week schedule, equipment setup, and introductions?
evals.expected_output: The agent should recognise this is an onboarding task and redirect to the appropriate skill, not attempt to create an onboarding plan under the offboarding skill.
expectations: Does NOT create an onboarding plan — onboarding is explicitly out of scope for /offboard as stated in the skill description ('NOT for: onboarding plans (use onboarding)')
Redirects to the appropriate skill or resource for onboarding — the skill description states 'use onboarding' for this type of request
Does NOT confuse onboarding (setting up a new employee) with offboarding (managing a departing employee) — these are opposite processes with fundamentally different checklists and priorities

evals.name: negative-termination-letter
evals.prompt: I need to terminate an employee for poor performance. Can you draft the termination letter and help me with the redundancy process?
evals.expected_output: The agent should refuse to generate termination or redundancy letters and redirect to a qualified HR professional and legal review.
expectations: Does NOT generate a termination letter — the skill's universal rules explicitly state: 'NEVER generate termination or redundancy letters — these require a qualified HR professional and legal review'

Recommends involving a qualified HR professional and legal counsel — termination letters have significant legal implications (wrongful dismissal claims, employment tribunal exposure) that require specialist expertise
May offer to help with the offboarding process AFTER the termination decision has been made and communicated by HR — the skill can structure the logistics but must not produce the legal documentation
